description: Phase 3: Decompose Tier 3 files (oauth.rs 857L, main.rs 836L, signals.rs 781L). Continues on the same branch. Note: exploration revealed US-009 and US-010 are smaller than PRD anticipated — oauth.rs has no browser/PKCE/server code, and main.rs has no centralized DB setup block. P2 completion verified: all 5 Tier 2 extractions done (watchdog.rs, status_queries.rs, status_display.rs, calibrate_math.rs, archive_display.rs; detection.rs EXPLICIT_SKIP with section markers). P2 final test count: 2264 passed.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
Test count is identical before and after extraction (run `cargo test 2>&1 | grep 'test result'`)
No breaking changes to existing public APIs — re-export if necessary
Every new module file has a //! module doc comment
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Map call graphs and test associations before moving any code
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Extraction compiles and all existing tests pass
3. CORRECTNESS - No orphaned tests, no broken imports, identical test count
4. CODE QUALITY - Module doc comments, pub(crate) visibility, alphabetical mod declarations
5. POLISH - Clean up unused imports in source file after extraction
principles: Move complete call chains together — if A calls B, extract both to same module
Tests move with their tested functions — never orphan a test
Verify cargo test count before and after each extraction
Use pub(crate) for internal APIs, pub only for cross-crate visibility

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

Verify Phase 2 modules exist (watchdog.rs, status_queries.rs, status_display.rs, calibrate_math.rs)

P2 added watchdog.rs, status_queries.rs, status_display.rs, calibrate_math.rs, archive_display.rs. detection.rs was reorganized in-place (EXPLICIT_SKIP). Verify all P2 modules exist before starting P3 extractions. P2 test count went 2262→2264 (2 new tests added in status modules).

acceptanceCriteria: Either: token_store.rs created with storage functions (credentials_path, read_credentials, write_credentials_atomic, is_token_expiring), Credentials struct, and their tests; oauth.rs keeps refresh_token, ensure_valid_token, TokenResult enum; mod.rs updated; external callers (engine.rs, usage.rs) import paths updated
Or: EXPLICIT_SKIP documented in progress.txt — production code is ~197 lines, only tests are large, split adds overhead without meaningful maintainability gain
In either case: cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description: Exploration revealed oauth.rs has NO browser/PKCE/callback server code — the PRD's 3-file split is not applicable. The file is 857 lines but ~660 are tests. Production code is ~197 lines with only 7 functions. The viable split is: token_store.rs (credentials_path, read_credentials, write_credentials_atomic, is_token_expiring — pure storage) vs oauth.rs (refresh_token, ensure_valid_token, sanitize_oauth_error — refresh flow). Alternatively, if the production code is only ~197 lines, document 'EXPLICIT_SKIP: production code is 197 lines, split not warranted' and mark complete.
userStories.notes: oauth.rs is 857 lines but 660 are tests. The 7 production functions total ~197 lines. The PRD assumed browser/PKCE/server code existed — it doesn't. Splitting ~100 lines of storage from ~100 lines of refresh creates two tiny modules. The agent should make a pragmatic call. External callers: engine.rs (ensure_valid_token), usage.rs (credentials_path, read_credentials, is_token_expiring, refresh_token). P2 LEARNING: detection.rs (1013L, ~18L extractable) was EXPLICIT_SKIP with section markers — same pragmatic pattern applies here. With only ~197L production code, EXPLICIT_SKIP is the strongly recommended outcome.

correctness: Test count identical before and after
External callers compile without changes or with updated imports

userStories.title: Split oauth.rs token storage from refresh flow

acceptanceCriteria: Either: Command arms grouped into dispatch helpers (e.g., dispatch_learning_commands, dispatch_loop_commands) in appropriate commands/ modules; main.rs match arms reduced to single-line helper calls; main.rs under 400 lines
Or: EXPLICIT_SKIP documented — each arm is already a clean 5-20 line block, no helper extraction warranted; document in progress.txt with rationale
get_project_root helper moved to db/connection.rs or kept in main.rs (agent's judgment)
DB setup extraction assessed — document finding that open_connection is called per-arm, no centralized DB setup block exists (PRD US-010 assumption was incorrect)
cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description: main.rs has a single 773-line match statement with 34 command arms. There is no centralized DB setup to extract (the PRD assumption was wrong — open_connection is called per-arm). The `run` subcommand handler is only ~30 lines. The real opportunity is grouping related command arms into dispatch helper functions in commands/ modules. Approach: (1) Group related arms into helper functions (e.g., learning commands, loop/batch commands, admin commands). (2) Each helper takes &Cli and the specific command variant. (3) main.rs becomes a thin dispatcher calling these helpers. Alternative: document EXPLICIT_SKIP if the match statement, while large, is already well-organized and each arm is a clear 5-20 line block.
userStories.explicitSkip: EXPLICIT_SKIP: 34 match arms are each clean 5-30 line blocks. Flat match is idiomatic Rust CLI dispatch. No centralized DB setup to extract (PRD assumption wrong). Dispatch helpers would add indirection without reducing complexity. No test module to move. Documented in progress.txt.
userStories.notes: main.rs has NO #[cfg(test)] module — no tests to move. get_project_root (L33-50) calls git rev-parse, used by Loop/Batch/Worktrees arms only. The 34 command arms are individually small (5-30 lines each) but collectively make main.rs hard to navigate. The PRD's goal of 'main.rs as thin routing layer' is achieved by grouping arms into dispatch helpers. This is a judgment call — flat match statements are idiomatic in Rust CLIs. P2 LEARNING: detection.rs was successfully reorganized in-place with section markers (// --- Section ---) instead of extraction. Consider section markers as an alternative to dispatch helpers if arms are already clean 5-20 line blocks — this is lower risk and idiomatic for Rust CLI match statements. EXPLICIT_SKIP with section markers is a valid P3 outcome.

correctness: No tests to move (main.rs has no test module)
All CLI commands produce identical output
style: Grouping follows existing command module structure
Each dispatch helper is a single-responsibility function

userStories.title: Extract main.rs command dispatch into grouped helpers

acceptanceCriteria: guidance.rs contains: SessionGuidance struct, GuidanceEntry struct, all SessionGuidance methods (new, add, format_for_prompt, is_empty, format_for_recording)
guidance.rs has //! module doc comment
signals.rs keeps: SignalFlag, all stop/pause/cleanup functions, handle_pause
handle_pause imports SessionGuidance from guidance module
engine.rs import updated: SessionGuidance from guidance instead of signals
mod.rs updated with `pub mod guidance;`
steering.md parsing assessed — document whether any steering.md file reading exists in signals.rs (PRD assumed it did; exploration suggests it doesn't)
Associated SessionGuidance tests (~130 lines, L309-441) moved to guidance.rs #[cfg(test)]
cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description: Move SessionGuidance struct and its impl block (~70 lines, L22-93) from signals.rs to a new src/loop_engine/guidance.rs module. SessionGuidance accumulates interactive stdin guidance during pause events — it is conceptually separate from signal file handling (stop/pause file checks). Also move GuidanceEntry (private struct, L28). Keep in signals.rs: SignalFlag, check_stop_signal, check_pause_signal, cleanup_signal_files, cleanup_deadline_files, handle_pause, stop_file_path, pause_file_path. Note: handle_pause (L153) takes &mut SessionGuidance — after extraction it imports from guidance.rs.
userStories.notes: PRD US-012 names target as 'steering.rs' — renamed to guidance.rs because exploration found NO steering.md file parsing in signals.rs. SessionGuidance accumulates interactive stdin guidance, not file-based steering. SessionGuidance is imported by name in engine.rs (L44: `use ...signals::{self, SessionGuidance, SignalFlag}`). After extraction, engine.rs imports SessionGuidance from guidance and SignalFlag from signals. handle_pause in signals.rs takes &mut SessionGuidance — it will import from super::guidance::SessionGuidance. The ~130 lines of SessionGuidance tests (L309-441) move; the remaining ~350 lines of signal tests stay. P2 LEARNINGS: (1) Re-export pattern (status.rs re-exports structs from status_queries/status_display) avoids changing external callers — consider re-exporting SessionGuidance from signals.rs if many callers import it. (2) Use pub(crate) for internal APIs, pub only for cross-crate. (3) Line numbers shift after P2 extractions — verify actual line numbers before editing. (4) Private `use` imports in parent module are NOT accessible via `use super::*` in test submodule — must add explicit imports in moved test code. (5) Struct fields must be pub(crate) if moved functions access them from a different module.

correctness: Test count identical before and after
SessionGuidance tests pass in new location
handle_pause still works with imported SessionGuidance
style: pub for SessionGuidance (used outside loop_engine via engine.rs)

userStories.title: Extract SessionGuidance from signals.rs to guidance.rs

userStories.description: Review all Phase 3 tasks for correct visibility, no orphaned tests, no unused imports, module doc comments, integration wiring.
userStories.notes: Some Phase 3 tasks may result in EXPLICIT_SKIP rather than extraction. Review that skip decisions are justified. P2 LEARNING: P2 code review found minor issues (wrong doc comment style, misplaced import) that were fixed in-place without CODE-FIX tasks. Reserve CODE-FIX tasks for issues requiring significant changes. Also verify pub vs pub(crate) consistency — P2 found TimeoutConfig was pub when it should have been pub(crate). P2 FINAL: Scope reviews to branch-changed code only — prevents scope creep while catching real issues.
